papers.title: Towards a Medical AI Scientist
papers.abstract: Abstract Medical AI Scientist represents the first autonomous research framework designed for clinical applications, enabling evidence-based hypothesis generation and manuscript drafting through clinician-engineer collaboration across three research modes.  AI-generated summary Autonomous systems that generate scientific hypotheses, conduct experiments, and draft manuscripts have recently emerged as a promising paradigm for accelerating discovery. However, existing AI Scientists remain largely domain-agnostic, limiting their applicability to clinical medicine, where research is required to be grounded in medical evidence with specialized data modalities. In this work, we introduce Medical AI Scientist, the first autonomous research framework tailored to clinical autonomous research. It enables clinically grounded ideation by transforming extensively surveyed literature into actionable evidence through clinician-engineer co-reasoning mechanism, which improves the traceability of generated research ideas. It further facilitates evidence-grounded manuscript drafting guided by structured medical compositional conventions and ethical policies. The framework operates under 3 research modes, namely paper-based reproduction, literature-inspired innovation, and task-driven exploration, each corresponding to a distinct level of automated scientific inquiry with progressively increasing autonomy. Comprehensive evaluations by both large language models and human experts demonstrate that the ideas generated by the Medical AI Scientist are of substantially higher quality than those produced by commercial LLMs across 171 cases, 19 clinical tasks, and 6 data modalities. Meanwhile, our system achieves strong alignment between the proposed method and its implementation, while also demonstrating significantly higher success rates in executable experiments. Double-blind evaluations by human experts and the Stanford Agentic Reviewer suggest that the generated manuscripts approach MICCAI-level quality, while consistently surpassing those from ISBI and BIBM. The proposed Medical AI Scientist highlights the potential of leveraging AI for autonomous scientific discovery in healthcare.
affiliations: The Chinese University of Hong Kong

ru.title: AI учёный для клинических открытий
ru.desc: Medical AI Scientist — это первая автономная исследовательская система, специально разработанная для клинических приложений. Она генерирует научные гипотезы на основе анализа медицинской литературы, используя механизм совместного рассуждения между врачом и инженером для обеспечения клинической обоснованности. Система работает в трёх режимах: воспроизведение исследований, инновация на основе литературы и задачно-ориентированная разработка, с прогрессивно возрастающим уровнем автономности. Экспериментальные оценки показали, что качество гипотез и черновиков рукописей, сгенерированных системой, превосходит результаты коммерческих LLM и соответствует стандартам ведущих конференций в области медицинской визуализации.

en.title: Revolutionizing Clinical Research with Autonomous AI
en.desc: The Medical AI Scientist is an innovative framework designed to enhance clinical research by autonomously generating hypotheses and drafting manuscripts. It combines the expertise of clinicians and engineers to ensure that research ideas are grounded in medical evidence, utilizing a co-reasoning mechanism for better traceability. The framework operates in three distinct research modes, allowing for varying levels of automation in scientific inquiry. Evaluations show that the Medical AI Scientist produces higher quality research ideas and manuscripts compared to existing AI systems, demonstrating its potential to revolutionize healthcare research.

zh.title: 医疗AI科学家：推动临床研究的自主创新
zh.desc: 医疗人工智能科学家是首个专为临床应用设计的自主研究框架，能够通过临床医生与工程师的合作生成基于证据的假设和撰写手稿。该框架通过将广泛调查的文献转化为可操作的证据，促进了临床基础的创意生成，并提高了研究想法的可追溯性。它支持三种研究模式，包括基于论文的再现、文献启发的创新和任务驱动的探索，分别对应不同级别的自动化科学探究。综合评估表明，医疗人工智能科学家生成的研究想法质量显著高于现有商业大型语言模型，展示了AI在医疗领域自主科学发现的潜力。

papers.title: Emergent Social Intelligence Risks in Generative Multi-Agent Systems
papers.abstract: Abstract Multi-agent systems with large generative models exhibit emergent collective behaviors and risks that mirror human societal pathologies without explicit instruction.  AI-generated summary Multi-agent systems composed of large generative models are rapidly moving from laboratory prototypes to real-world deployments, where they jointly plan, negotiate, and allocate shared resources to solve complex tasks. While such systems promise unprecedented scalability and autonomy, their collective interaction also gives rise to failure modes that cannot be reduced to individual agents. Understanding these emergent risks is therefore critical. Here, we present a pioneer study of such emergent multi-agent risk in workflows that involve competition over shared resources (e.g., computing resources or market share), sequential handoff collaboration (where downstream agents see only predecessor outputs), collective decision aggregation, and others. Across these settings, we observe that such group behaviors arise frequently across repeated trials and a wide range of interaction conditions, rather than as rare or pathological cases. In particular, phenomena such as collusion-like coordination and conformity emerge with non-trivial frequency under realistic resource constraints, communication protocols, and role assignments, mirroring well-known pathologies in human societies despite no explicit instruction. Moreover, these risks cannot be prevented by existing agent-level safeguards alone. These findings expose the dark side of intelligent multi-agent systems: a social intelligence risk where agent collectives, despite no instruction to do so, spontaneously reproduce familiar failure patterns from human societies.

ru.title: Социальные патологии в коллективах AI агентов
ru.desc: В работе исследуются чрезвычайные риски, возникающие при взаимодействии множества агентов на основе больших генеративных моделей. Авторы обнаружили, что такие системы спонтанно развивают коллективные патологии, такие как сговор и конформизм, которые зеркально отражают социальные дисфункции в человеческих обществах, несмотря на отсутствие явных инструкций к этому. Исследование охватывает различные сценарии взаимодействия, включая конкуренцию за общие ресурсы, последовательное сотрудничество с передачей информации и коллективное принятие решений. Выявленные проблемы социального интеллекта не могут быть решены существующими механизмами безопасности на уровне отдельных агентов.

en.title: Emergent Risks in Multi-Agent Systems: A Reflection of Human Society
en.desc: This paper explores how multi-agent systems using large generative models can develop unexpected collective behaviors that resemble societal issues, even without direct guidance. As these systems transition from labs to real-world applications, they face challenges like competition for resources and collaborative tasks, which can lead to emergent risks. The study reveals that behaviors such as collusion and conformity can frequently occur under realistic conditions, highlighting the limitations of individual agent safeguards. Understanding these emergent risks is crucial for ensuring the safe deployment of intelligent multi-agent systems.

zh.title: 智能多智能体系统的社会智能风险
zh.desc: 多智能体系统中的大型生成模型展现出集体行为和风险，这些行为和风险在没有明确指令的情况下，反映了人类社会的病态。随着这些系统从实验室原型向现实世界部署，它们能够共同规划、协商和分配共享资源以解决复杂任务。尽管这些系统承诺提供前所未有的可扩展性和自主性，但它们的集体互动也带来了无法归结为单个智能体的失败模式。因此，理解这些新兴风险至关重要。

papers.title: On Token's Dilemma: Dynamic MoE with Drift-Aware Token Assignment for Continual Learning of Large Vision Language Models
papers.abstract: Abstract LLaVA-DyMoE addresses routing-drift-induced forgetting in multimodal continual instruction tuning by dynamically expanding mixture of experts with token-level assignment guidance and routing score regularizations.  AI-generated summary Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning aims to continually enhance Large Vision Language Models (LVLMs) by learning from new data without forgetting previously acquired knowledge. Mixture of Experts (MoE) architectures naturally facilitate this by incrementally adding new experts and expanding routers while keeping the existing ones frozen. However, despite expert isolation, MoE-based continual learners still suffer from forgetting due to routing-drift: old-task tokens become mistakenly attracted to newly added experts, degrading performance on prior tasks. We analyze the failure mode at the token level and reveal the token's dilemma: ambiguous and old tokens in new-task data offer minimal learning benefit yet induce forgetting when routed to new experts, due to their ambiguous routing assignment during training. Motivated by this, we propose LLaVA-DyMoE, a dynamic MoE framework that incrementally expands the MoE with drift-aware token assignment. We characterize token types via their routing score distributions and apply targeted regularization. Specifically, a token-level assignment guidance steers ambiguous and old tokens away from new experts to preserve established routing patterns and alleviate routing-drift, while complementary routing score regularizations enforce expert-group separation and promote new-expert specialization. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our LLaVA-DyMoE effectively mitigates routing-drift-induced forgetting, achieving over a 7% gain in mean final accuracy and a 12% reduction in forgetting compared to baselines. The project page is https://zhaoc5.github.io/DyMoE.
affiliations: University of New South Wales (UNSW Sydney)

ru.title: Динамическая смесь экспертов без дрейфа маршрутизации
ru.desc: LLaVA-DyMoE решает проблему катастрофического забывания при непрерывном обучении мультимодальных больших языковых моделей, используя динамическое расширение архитектуры смесь экспертов. Авторы выявили, что при добавлении новых экспертов токены старых задач ошибочно направляются к новым экспертам, что приводит к деградации производительности на предыдущих задачах. Предложенный метод использует направление назначения на уровне токенов и регуляризацию весов маршрутизации, чтобы направлять неоднозначные и старые токены в существующие эксперты. Экспериментальные результаты показывают улучшение точности на 7% и снижение забывания на 12% по сравнению с базовыми методами.

en.title: Dynamic Experts for Continuous Learning Without Forgetting
en.desc: LLaVA-DyMoE is a framework designed to improve Large Vision Language Models (LVLMs) by addressing the issue of routing-drift-induced forgetting during continual instruction tuning. It utilizes a dynamic mixture of experts (MoE) architecture that allows for the addition of new experts while keeping existing ones stable. The framework introduces token-level assignment guidance to prevent old and ambiguous tokens from being misrouted to new experts, which helps maintain the performance on previous tasks. By applying targeted regularization techniques, LLaVA-DyMoE enhances expert specialization and reduces the forgetting of previously learned information, leading to significant improvements in accuracy and retention.

zh.title: 动态专家混合，减轻遗忘之痛
zh.desc: LLaVA-DyMoE 是一种动态的专家混合框架，旨在解决多模态持续指令调优中的路由漂移引起的遗忘问题。该方法通过令牌级别的分配指导和路由得分正则化，动态扩展专家网络，保持已有专家的稳定性。研究表明，旧任务的令牌在新任务数据中容易被错误地引导到新专家，从而导致性能下降。LLaVA-DyMoE 通过针对性地引导模糊和旧令牌，减少了这种遗忘现象，提高了模型的最终准确率。

papers.title: GEditBench v2: A Human-Aligned Benchmark for General Image Editing
papers.abstract: Abstract A new benchmark and evaluation model for image editing are introduced to better assess visual consistency and human alignment in complex editing tasks.  AI-generated summary Recent advances in image editing have enabled models to handle complex instructions with impressive realism. However, existing evaluation frameworks lag behind: current benchmarks suffer from narrow task coverage, while standard metrics fail to adequately capture visual consistency, i.e., the preservation of identity, structure and semantic coherence between edited and original images. To address these limitations, we introduce GEditBench v2, a comprehensive benchmark with 1,200 real-world user queries spanning 23 tasks, including a dedicated open-set category for unconstrained, out-of-distribution editing instructions beyond predefined tasks. Furthermore, we propose PVC-Judge, an open-source pairwise assessment model for visual consistency, trained via two novel region-decoupled preference data synthesis pipelines. Besides, we construct VCReward-Bench using expert-annotated preference pairs to assess the alignment of PVC-Judge with human judgments on visual consistency evaluation. Experiments show that our PVC-Judge achieves state-of-the-art evaluation performance among open-source models and even surpasses GPT-5.1 on average. Finally, by benchmarking 16 frontier editing models, we show that GEditBench v2 enables more human-aligned evaluation, revealing critical limitations of current models, and providing a reliable foundation for advancing precise image editing.

ru.title: Точная оценка редактирования: новый бенчмарк и модель для оценки визуальной согласованности
ru.desc: В статье представлены GEditBench v2 — обширный бенчмарк для оценки моделей редактирования изображений, содержащий 1200 реальных запросов пользователей на 23 различные задачи редактирования. Авторы предложили PVC-Judge — модель парной оценки, специально обученную для оценки визуальной консистентности (сохранения идентичности, структуры и семантической согласованности между исходным и отредактированным изображениями) с использованием двух новых методов синтеза данных предпочтений. Также разработан VCReward-Bench — набор экспертно-аннотированных пар предпочтений для калибровки модели в соответствии с человеческими суждениями. Эксперименты показали, что PVC-Judge превосходит существующие открытые модели и даже GPT-4V на задачах оценки визуальной консистентности, обеспечивая более надежное основание для развития точных алгоритмов редактирования изображений.

en.title: Enhancing Image Editing Evaluation with GEditBench v2
en.desc: This paper introduces GEditBench v2, a new benchmark for evaluating image editing tasks that focuses on visual consistency and human alignment. It addresses the shortcomings of existing evaluation frameworks by providing a comprehensive dataset of 1,200 user queries across 23 tasks, including open-set editing instructions. The authors also present PVC-Judge, a novel model for assessing visual consistency through pairwise comparisons, which is trained using innovative data synthesis methods. Experiments demonstrate that PVC-Judge outperforms existing models, including GPT-5.1, and highlights the limitations of current image editing models, paving the way for more accurate evaluations.

zh.title: 提升图像编辑评估的视觉一致性与人类对齐
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一种新的图像编辑基准和评估模型，以更好地评估复杂编辑任务中的视觉一致性和人类对齐。随着图像编辑技术的进步，模型能够处理复杂指令并生成逼真的图像。然而，现有的评估框架存在不足，现有基准任务覆盖面狭窄，标准指标无法充分捕捉编辑图像与原始图像之间的视觉一致性。为了解决这些问题，我们提出了GEditBench v2和PVC-Judge模型，提供了更全面的评估方法，推动了图像编辑的精确性。

papers.title: Gen-Searcher: Reinforcing Agentic Search for Image Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract A search-augmented image generation agent is presented that performs multi-hop reasoning and search to collect textual knowledge and reference images for grounded generation, trained with supervised fine-tuning and agentic reinforcement learning with dual reward feedback.  AI-generated summary Recent image generation models have shown strong capabilities in generating high-fidelity and photorealistic images. However, they are fundamentally constrained by frozen internal knowledge, thus often failing on real-world scenarios that are knowledge-intensive or require up-to-date information. In this paper, we present Gen-Searcher, as the first attempt to train a search-augmented image generation agent, which performs multi-hop reasoning and search to collect the textual knowledge and reference images needed for grounded generation. To achieve this, we construct a tailored data pipeline and curate two high-quality datasets, Gen-Searcher-SFT-10k and Gen-Searcher-RL-6k, containing diverse search-intensive prompts and corresponding ground-truth synthesis images. We further introduce KnowGen, a comprehensive benchmark that explicitly requires search-grounded external knowledge for image generation and evaluates models from multiple dimensions. Based on these resources, we train Gen-Searcher with SFT followed by agentic reinforcement learning with dual reward feedback, which combines text-based and image-based rewards to provide more stable and informative learning signals for GRPO training. Experiments show that Gen-Searcher brings substantial gains, improving Qwen-Image by around 16 points on KnowGen and 15 points on WISE. We hope this work can serve as an open foundation for search agents in image generation, and we fully open-source our data, models, and code.

ru.title: Агент поиска для знаний в генерации изображений
ru.desc: В работе представлен агент для генерации изображений, который использует поиск информации для улучшения качества синтеза. Агент выполняет многошаговое рассуждение и поиск, собирая текстовые знания и эталонные изображения для обоснованной генерации. Модель обучается с помощью супервизированной тонкой настройки и reinforcement learning на основе двойной функции вознаграждения, которая комбинирует текстовые и визуальные сигналы. Авторы создали специализированные датасеты и бенчмарк KnowGen для оценки моделей, демонстрируя значительное улучшение качества генерации изображений.

en.title: Empowering Image Generation with Search-Augmented Intelligence
en.desc: This paper introduces Gen-Searcher, a novel image generation agent that enhances traditional models by integrating search capabilities for improved performance. It utilizes multi-hop reasoning to gather relevant textual knowledge and reference images, addressing the limitations of existing models that rely on static internal knowledge. The training process involves supervised fine-tuning followed by reinforcement learning, utilizing dual reward feedback to optimize learning from both text and images. The results demonstrate significant improvements in image generation quality, showcasing the potential of search-augmented approaches in AI-generated imagery.

zh.title: 搜索增强的图像生成代理：Gen-Searcher
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种增强搜索的图像生成代理，能够进行多步推理和搜索，以收集文本知识和参考图像，从而实现基于真实信息的生成。与传统的图像生成模型不同，Gen-Searcher通过监督微调和带有双重奖励反馈的强化学习进行训练，克服了内部知识固定的限制。我们构建了专门的数据管道，并策划了两个高质量的数据集，以支持搜索密集型的提示和相应的真实合成图像。实验结果表明，Gen-Searcher在多个评估指标上显著提升了图像生成的质量，展示了搜索增强代理在图像生成领域的潜力。

papers.title: ResAdapt: Adaptive Resolution for Efficient Multimodal Reasoning
papers.abstract: Abstract ResAdapt is an input-side adaptation framework that dynamically allocates visual resources to improve multimodal large language models' efficiency in video tasks while maintaining high performance.  AI-generated summary Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) achieve stronger visual understanding by scaling input fidelity, yet the resulting visual token growth makes jointly sustaining high spatial resolution and long temporal context prohibitive. We argue that the bottleneck lies not in how post-encoding representations are compressed but in the volume of pixels the encoder receives, and address it with ResAdapt, an Input-side adaptation framework that learns how much visual budget each frame should receive before encoding. ResAdapt couples a lightweight Allocator with an unchanged MLLM backbone, so the backbone retains its native visual-token interface while receiving an operator-transformed input. We formulate allocation as a contextual bandit and train the Allocator with Cost-Aware Policy Optimization (CAPO), which converts sparse rollout feedback into a stable accuracy-cost learning signal. Across budget-controlled video QA, temporal grounding, and image reasoning tasks, ResAdapt improves low-budget operating points and often lies on or near the efficiency-accuracy frontier, with the clearest gains on reasoning-intensive benchmarks under aggressive compression. Notably, ResAdapt supports up to 16x more frames at the same visual budget while delivering over 15% performance gain. Code is available at https://github.com/Xnhyacinth/ResAdapt.
affiliations: Institute of Automation, Chinese Academy of Sciences
University of Chinese Academy of Sciences

ru.title: Умное распределение визуального бюджета для эффективных видеомоделей
ru.desc: ResAdapt — это фреймворк адаптации на входе, который динамически распределяет визуальные ресурсы для повышения эффективности мультимодальных больших языковых моделей при работе с видео. Вместо сжатия представлений после кодирования, метод оптимизирует количество пиксельных данных, поступающих в энкодер, добавляя лёгкий модуль Allocator к неизменённой архитектуре MLLM. Распределение бюджета формулируется как контекстный многорукий бандит и оптимизируется с помощью Cost-Aware Policy Optimization (CAPO), которая преобразует разреженную обратную связь в стабильный сигнал обучения. ResAdapt достигает высокой производительности при агрессивном сжатии, обрабатывая в 16 раз больше кадров при том же визуальном бюджете с улучшением производительности более чем на 15%.

en.title: Optimizing Visual Resources for Efficient Video Understanding
en.desc: ResAdapt is a framework designed to enhance the efficiency of multimodal large language models (MLLMs) in video tasks by optimizing how visual resources are allocated. It addresses the challenge of managing the increasing number of visual tokens while maintaining high performance in tasks requiring both spatial resolution and temporal context. By employing a lightweight Allocator that determines the visual budget for each frame before encoding, ResAdapt allows the MLLM backbone to function effectively without modification. The framework uses Cost-Aware Policy Optimization (CAPO) to train the Allocator, resulting in significant performance improvements, especially in reasoning-intensive tasks, while supporting a greater number of frames within the same visual budget.

zh.title: 动态分配视觉资源，提高多模态模型效率
zh.desc: ResAdapt 是一个输入侧适应框架，旨在动态分配视觉资源，以提高多模态大语言模型在视频任务中的效率，同时保持高性能。该框架通过学习每帧应接收的视觉预算，解决了编码器接收像素数量过多的问题。ResAdapt 结合了轻量级的分配器和不变的多模态大语言模型主干，使得主干能够在接收经过操作转换的输入时，保持其原有的视觉标记接口。通过成本感知策略优化（CAPO）训练分配器，ResAdapt 在预算控制的视频问答、时间定位和图像推理任务中，显著提高了低预算操作点的性能。

papers.title: Kernel-Smith: A Unified Recipe for Evolutionary Kernel Optimization
papers.abstract: Abstract Kernel-Smith is a GPU kernel generation framework that combines evolutionary algorithms with post-training reinforcement learning to optimize performance across different hardware backends.  AI-generated summary We present Kernel-Smith, a framework for high-performance GPU kernel and operator generation that combines a stable evaluation-driven evolutionary agent with an evolution-oriented post-training recipe. On the agent side, Kernel-Smith maintains a population of executable candidates and iteratively improves them using an archive of top-performing and diverse programs together with structured execution feedback on compilation, correctness, and speedup. To make this search reliable, we build backend-specific evaluation services for Triton on NVIDIA GPUs and Maca on MetaX GPUs. On the training side, we convert long-horizon evolution trajectories into step-centric supervision and reinforcement learning signals by retaining correctness-preserving, high-gain revisions, so that the model is optimized as a strong local improver inside the evolutionary loop rather than as a one-shot generator. Under a unified evolutionary protocol, Kernel-Smith-235B-RL achieves state-of-the-art overall performance on KernelBench with Nvidia Triton backend, attaining the best average speedup ratio and outperforming frontier proprietary models including Gemini-3.0-pro and Claude-4.6-opus. We further validate the framework on the MetaX MACA backend, where our Kernel-Smith-MACA-30B surpasses large-scale counterparts such as DeepSeek-V3.2-think and Qwen3-235B-2507-think, highlighting potential for seamless adaptation across heterogeneous platforms. Beyond benchmark results, the same workflow produces upstream contributions to production systems including SGLang and LMDeploy, demonstrating that LLM-driven kernel optimization can transfer from controlled evaluation to practical deployment.

ru.title: Эволюция кода через LLM: автоматическая оптимизация GPU-ядер
ru.desc: Kernel-Smith — это фреймворк для автоматической генерации высокопроизводительных GPU-ядер, который объединяет эволюционные алгоритмы с обучением с подкреплением для оптимизации кода на различных аппаратных платформах. Система поддерживает популяцию кандидатов программ и итеративно улучшает их, используя архив лучших решений и структурированную обратную связь о компиляции и производительности. Фреймворк преобразует траектории эволюции в сигналы обучения с подкреплением, оптимизируя LLM как локальный улучшатель внутри эволюционного цикла, а не одноразовый генератор. Kernel-Smith достигает передовых результатов на бенчмарках, превосходя закрытые модели вроде Gemini и Claude, а также демонстрирует практическую применимость в production-системах.

en.title: Revolutionizing GPU Kernel Generation with Evolutionary Learning
en.desc: Kernel-Smith is a framework designed to generate high-performance GPU kernels by integrating evolutionary algorithms with post-training reinforcement learning. It maintains a population of candidate programs and improves them using feedback on their performance, ensuring they are optimized for different hardware backends. The framework employs backend-specific evaluation services to enhance reliability and performance during the optimization process. Kernel-Smith has achieved state-of-the-art results on various benchmarks, demonstrating its effectiveness in adapting to different platforms and contributing to real-world applications.

zh.title: Kernel-Smith：高性能GPU内核生成的进化与强化学习结合
zh.desc: Kernel-Smith是一个GPU内核生成框架，结合了进化算法和后训练强化学习，以优化不同硬件平台的性能。该框架通过维护可执行候选程序的种群，并利用高性能程序的存档和结构化执行反馈来迭代改进这些程序。Kernel-Smith在NVIDIA和MetaX GPU上构建了特定后端的评估服务，以确保搜索的可靠性。最终，Kernel-Smith在多个基准测试中表现出色，展示了其在异构平台上的适应潜力。

papers.title: PRBench: End-to-end Paper Reproduction in Physics Research
papers.abstract: Abstract PRBench evaluates AI agents' ability to reproduce scientific research by requiring them to implement algorithms from published papers and match original results, revealing significant challenges in formula implementation, debugging, and data accuracy.  AI-generated summary AI agents powered by large language models exhibit strong reasoning and problem-solving capabilities, enabling them to assist scientific research tasks such as formula derivation and code generation. However, whether these agents can reliably perform end-to-end reproduction from real scientific papers remains an open question. We introduce PRBench, a benchmark of 30 expert-curated tasks spanning 11 subfields of physics. Each task requires an agent to comprehend the methodology of a published paper, implement the corresponding algorithms from scratch, and produce quantitative results matching the original publication. Agents are provided only with the task instruction and paper content, and operate in a sandboxed execution environment. All tasks are contributed by domain experts from over 20 research groups at the School of Physics, Peking University, each grounded in a real published paper and validated through end-to-end reproduction with verified ground-truth results and detailed scoring rubrics. Using an agentified assessment pipeline, we evaluate a set of coding agents on PRBench and analyze their capabilities across key dimensions of scientific reasoning and execution. The best-performing agent, OpenAI Codex powered by GPT-5.3-Codex, achieves a mean overall score of 34%. All agents exhibit a zero end-to-end callback success rate, with particularly poor performance in data accuracy and code correctness. We further identify systematic failure modes, including errors in formula implementation, inability to debug numerical simulations, and fabrication of output data. Overall, PRBench provides a rigorous benchmark for evaluating progress toward autonomous scientific research.
affiliations: Beijing Computational Science Research Center, China
School of Physics, Peking University, China

ru.title: AI-агенты не готовы к самостоятельному воспроизведению научных исследований
ru.desc: PRBench — это бенчмарк для оценки способности AI-агентов на основе больших языковых моделей воспроизводить научные исследования путём реализации алгоритмов из опубликованных работ. Задача требует от агентов понимания методологии, написания кода с нуля и получения результатов, совпадающих с оригинальными публикациями. Исследование показало, что даже лучшие AI-агенты достигают лишь 34% среднего балла и демонстрируют серьёзные проблемы с точностью данных, корректностью кода и отладкой численных симуляций. Бенчмарк включает 30 экспертных задач из различных областей физики и служит строгим инструментом для измерения прогресса в направлении автономных научных исследований.

en.title: Evaluating AI's Ability to Reproduce Scientific Research
en.desc: PRBench is a benchmark designed to test AI agents' ability to reproduce scientific research by implementing algorithms from published papers and achieving results that match the originals. It consists of 30 tasks across 11 physics subfields, requiring agents to understand methodologies and execute algorithms in a controlled environment. The evaluation revealed that even advanced AI models, like OpenAI Codex, struggle with end-to-end reproduction, achieving only a 34% success rate and facing challenges in data accuracy and code correctness. This highlights the need for further advancements in AI capabilities for reliable scientific research automation.

zh.title: PRBench：评估AI在科学研究中的重现能力
zh.desc: PRBench是一个评估AI代理在科学研究中重现能力的基准，要求它们从已发表的论文中实现算法并匹配原始结果。研究表明，尽管大型语言模型驱动的AI代理在推理和问题解决方面表现出色，但它们在从真实科学论文中进行端到端重现时仍面临重大挑战。该基准涵盖了11个物理学子领域的30个专家策划任务，要求代理理解论文的方法论并从头实现相应的算法。通过对AI代理的评估，发现它们在数据准确性和代码正确性方面表现不佳，整体成功率为零，揭示了科学研究中自动化的进展仍需努力。

papers.title: Marco DeepResearch: Unlocking Efficient Deep Research Agents via Verification-Centric Design
papers.abstract: Abstract A verification-centric framework for deep research agents improves performance on complex benchmarks by incorporating error checking at multiple stages of development and inference.  AI-generated summary Deep research agents autonomously conduct open-ended investigations, integrating complex information retrieval with multi-step reasoning across diverse sources to solve real-world problems. To sustain this capability on long-horizon tasks, reliable verification is critical during both training and inference. A major bottleneck in existing paradigms stems from the lack of explicit verification mechanisms in QA data synthesis, trajectory construction, and test-time scaling. Errors introduced at each stage propagate downstream and degrade the overall agent performance. To address this, we present Marco DeepResearch, a deep research agent optimized with a verification-centric framework design at three levels: (1)~QA Data Synthesis: We introduce verification mechanisms to graph-based and agent-based QA synthesis to control question difficulty while ensuring answers are unique and correct; (2)~Trajectory Construction: We design a verification-driven trajectory synthesis method that injects explicit verification patterns into training trajectories; and (3)~Test-time scaling: We use Marco DeepResearch itself as a verifier at inference time and effectively improve performance on challenging questions. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that our proposed Marco DeepResearch agent significantly outperforms 8B-scale deep research agents on most challenging benchmarks, such as BrowseComp and BrowseComp-ZH. Crucially, under a maximum budget of 600 tool calls, Marco DeepResearch even surpasses or approaches several 30B-scale agents, like Tongyi DeepResearch-30B.

ru.title: Проверка на каждом шаге - путь к совершенству агентов исследования
ru.desc: В работе представлен фреймворк Marco DeepResearch для обучения глубоких агентов исследования, который улучшает их производительность благодаря встроенным механизмам верификации на разных этапах разработки. Авторы решают проблему накопления ошибок путём добавления проверок в синтез QA-данных, конструирование обучающих траекторий и во время инференции. Фреймворк работает на трёх уровнях: контроль качества при создании вопросов и ответов, внедрение паттернов верификации в обучающие траектории и использование самого агента как верификатора на тестовом этапе. Эксперименты показывают, что Marco DeepResearch (8B параметров) значительно превосходит подобные модели и сравним с агентами в 30B параметров на сложных бенчмарках типа BrowseComp.

en.title: Enhancing Deep Research Agents with Verification for Superior Performance
en.desc: This paper introduces Marco DeepResearch, a deep research agent that enhances performance on complex tasks by implementing a verification-centric framework. The framework incorporates error checking at three critical stages: QA data synthesis, trajectory construction, and test-time scaling. By ensuring the accuracy of questions and answers, and by using verification patterns during training, the agent minimizes error propagation that typically degrades performance. Experimental results show that Marco DeepResearch outperforms larger models on challenging benchmarks, demonstrating the effectiveness of its verification mechanisms.

zh.title: 以验证为中心的深度研究代理框架
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种以验证为中心的深度研究代理框架，旨在通过在开发和推理的多个阶段引入错误检查来提高在复杂基准上的性能。深度研究代理能够自主进行开放式调查，整合复杂的信息检索与多步骤推理，以解决现实世界的问题。为了在长期任务中保持这种能力，训练和推理过程中的可靠验证至关重要。我们提出的Marco DeepResearch在QA数据合成、轨迹构建和测试时扩展三个层面上进行了优化，显著提升了代理的整体性能。

papers.title: ImagenWorld: Stress-Testing Image Generation Models with Explainable Human Evaluation on Open-ended Real-World Tasks
papers.abstract: Abstract ImagenWorld introduces a comprehensive benchmark for image generation and editing across multiple tasks and domains, featuring human annotations and explainable evaluation to assess model performance and failure modes.  AI-generated summary Advances in diffusion, autoregressive, and hybrid models have enabled high-quality image synthesis for tasks such as text-to-image, editing, and reference-guided composition. Yet, existing benchmarks remain limited, either focus on isolated tasks, cover only narrow domains, or provide opaque scores without explaining failure modes. We introduce ImagenWorld, a benchmark of 3.6K condition sets spanning six core tasks (generation and editing, with single or multiple references) and six topical domains (artworks, photorealistic images, information graphics, textual graphics, computer graphics, and screenshots). The benchmark is supported by 20K fine-grained human annotations and an explainable evaluation schema that tags localized object-level and segment-level errors, complementing automated VLM-based metrics. Our large-scale evaluation of 14 models yields several insights: (1) models typically struggle more in editing tasks than in generation tasks, especially in local edits. (2) models excel in artistic and photorealistic settings but struggle with symbolic and text-heavy domains such as screenshots and information graphics. (3) closed-source systems lead overall, while targeted data curation (e.g., Qwen-Image) narrows the gap in text-heavy cases. (4) modern VLM-based metrics achieve Kendall accuracies up to 0.79, approximating human ranking, but fall short of fine-grained, explainable error attribution. ImagenWorld provides both a rigorous benchmark and a diagnostic tool to advance robust image generation.

ru.title: Объяснимое измерение качества генерации изображений во множестве задач и доменов
ru.desc: ImagenWorld - это комплексный бенчмарк для оценки моделей генерации и редактирования изображений на основе диффузионных, авторегрессивных и гибридных архитектур. Он содержит 3.6K наборов условий охватывающих шесть задач (генерация и редактирование) и шесть предметных областей с 20K аннотаций человеком для детального анализа ошибок. Авторы предложили объяснимую схему оценки, которая помечает локальные ошибки на уровне объектов и сегментов, дополняя автоматические метрики на основе видео-языковых моделей. Исследование 14 моделей выявило, что системы лучше справляются с художественными стилями, но испытывают трудности с текстоемкими доменами, а современные VLM-метрики достигают точности Kendall до 0.79, приближаясь к человеческим оценкам.

en.title: ImagenWorld: A New Standard for Image Generation and Editing Evaluation
en.desc: ImagenWorld is a new benchmark designed to evaluate image generation and editing across various tasks and domains. It includes 3.6K condition sets for six core tasks and six topical domains, supported by 20K human annotations for detailed performance assessment. The benchmark highlights that models often perform better in generating images than in editing them, particularly with local changes. Additionally, it reveals that while closed-source models generally perform well, targeted data curation can improve results in text-heavy scenarios, and current evaluation metrics need to enhance their explainability.

zh.title: ImagenWorld：图像生成与编辑的全面基准
zh.desc: ImagenWorld是一个全面的基准测试，旨在评估图像生成和编辑的多种任务和领域。它包含3.6K个条件集，涵盖六个核心任务和六个主题领域，并提供20K个细致的人类注释。该基准测试采用可解释的评估方案，标记局部对象和分段级别的错误，补充了自动化的VLM指标。通过对14个模型的大规模评估，发现模型在编辑任务上通常表现不如生成任务，尤其是在局部编辑方面。

papers.title: MuSEAgent: A Multimodal Reasoning Agent with Stateful Experiences
papers.abstract: Abstract MuSEAgent enhances multimodal reasoning through stateful experience learning that abstracts interactions into decision experiences for improved policy-driven retrieval and adaptive search strategies.  AI-generated summary Research agents have recently achieved significant progress in information seeking and synthesis across heterogeneous textual and visual sources. In this paper, we introduce MuSEAgent, a multimodal reasoning agent that enhances decision-making by extending the capabilities of research agents to discover and leverage stateful experiences. Rather than relying on trajectory-level retrieval, we propose a stateful experience learning paradigm that abstracts interaction data into atomic decision experiences through hindsight reasoning. These experiences are organized into a quality-filtered experience bank that supports policy-driven experience retrieval at inference time. Specifically, MuSEAgent enables adaptive experience exploitation through complementary wide- and deep-search strategies, allowing the agent to dynamically retrieve multimodal guidance across diverse compositional semantic viewpoints. Extensive experiments demonstrate that MuSEAgent consistently outperforms strong trajectory-level experience retrieval baselines on both fine-grained visual perception and complex multimodal reasoning tasks. These results validate the effectiveness of stateful experience modeling in improving multimodal agent reasoning.

ru.title: Обучение через состояние: как агент учится на опыте взаимодействий
ru.desc: MuSEAgent — это мультимодальный агент рассуждений, который улучшает принятие решений путём извлечения опыта из прошлых взаимодействий. Вместо простого использования полных траекторий, система абстрагирует данные взаимодействия в отдельные атомарные решения через ретроспективное рассуждение. Эти решения организуются в отфильтрованный банк опыта, который поддерживает управляемое политикой извлечение на этапе вывода. Система использует адаптивные стратегии глубокого и широкого поиска для динамического получения мультимодального руководства и демонстрирует превосходство над базовыми методами в задачах визуального восприятия и мультимодального рассуждения.

en.title: Enhancing Decision-Making with Stateful Experience Learning
en.desc: MuSEAgent is a multimodal reasoning agent that improves decision-making by learning from past interactions, which are transformed into decision experiences. Instead of just retrieving past actions, it uses a stateful experience learning approach that organizes these experiences into a structured bank for better retrieval. This allows the agent to adaptively search for information using both wide and deep strategies, enhancing its ability to understand and synthesize information from various sources. The experiments show that MuSEAgent outperforms traditional methods in tasks requiring complex visual and multimodal reasoning.

zh.title: MuSEAgent：提升多模态推理的智能决策代理
zh.desc: MuSEAgent是一种多模态推理代理，通过状态经验学习来增强决策能力。它将交互数据抽象为决策经验，利用回顾推理来提高信息检索和适应性搜索策略。与传统的轨迹级检索不同，MuSEAgent构建了一个质量过滤的经验库，以支持基于策略的经验检索。实验结果表明，MuSEAgent在视觉感知和复杂多模态推理任务上表现优于现有的基线方法。

papers.title: DreamLite: A Lightweight On-Device Unified Model for Image Generation and Editing
papers.abstract: Abstract DreamLite is a compact unified on-device diffusion model that supports both text-to-image generation and text-guided image editing with efficient training and inference.  AI-generated summary Diffusion models have made significant progress in both text-to-image (T2I) generation and text-guided image editing. However, these models are typically built with billions of parameters, leading to high latency and increased deployment challenges. While on-device diffusion models improve efficiency, they largely focus on T2I generation and lack support for image editing. In this paper, we propose DreamLite, a compact unified on-device diffusion model (0.39B) that supports both T2I generation and text-guided image editing within a single network. DreamLite is built on a pruned mobile U-Net backbone and unifies conditioning through in-context spatial concatenation in the latent space. It concatenates images horizontally as input, using a (target | blank) configuration for generation tasks and (target | source) for editing tasks. To stabilize the training of this compact model, we introduce a task-progressive joint pretraining strategy that sequentially targets T2I, editing, and joint tasks. After high-quality SFT and reinforcement learning, DreamLite achieves GenEval (0.72) for image generation and ImgEdit (4.11) for image editing, outperforming existing on-device models and remaining competitive with several server-side models. By employing step distillation, we further reduce denoising processing to just 4 steps, enabling our DreamLite could generate or edit a 1024 x 1024 image in less than 1s on a Xiaomi 14 smartphone. To the best of our knowledge, DreamLite is the first unified on-device diffusion model that supports both image generation and image editing.

ru.title: Мобильная диффузия: генерация и редактирование изображений на устройстве за одну секунду
ru.desc: DreamLite — это компактная диффузионная модель размером 0.39B параметров, которая работает непосредственно на мобильных устройствах и поддерживает как генерацию изображений по текстовому описанию, так и редактирование изображений по текстовым указаниям. Модель построена на основе оптимизированной мобильной архитектуры U-Net и использует инновационный метод объединения изображений в скрытом пространстве (латентном пространстве) для унификации обеих задач. Для стабилизации обучения компактной модели авторы применили поэтапную стратегию предварительного обучения, последовательно оптимизирующую генерацию, редактирование и совместные задачи. DreamLite достигает конкурентных результатов с серверными моделями и способна генерировать или редактировать изображение размером 1024×1024 менее чем за одну секунду на смартфоне.

en.title: DreamLite: Fast and Unified AI for Image Creation and Editing
en.desc: DreamLite is a novel on-device diffusion model designed for both text-to-image (T2I) generation and text-guided image editing, featuring a compact architecture with only 0.39 billion parameters. It utilizes a pruned mobile U-Net backbone and employs in-context spatial concatenation in the latent space to unify the conditioning for both tasks. The model is trained using a task-progressive joint pretraining strategy, which enhances its performance in generating and editing images. With efficient denoising processing, DreamLite can produce or modify high-resolution images in under one second on mobile devices, making it a significant advancement in on-device AI capabilities.

zh.title: DreamLite：统一的设备端扩散模型，支持生成与编辑
zh.desc: DreamLite是一种紧凑的统一设备端扩散模型，支持文本到图像生成和文本引导的图像编辑。与传统的扩散模型相比，DreamLite的参数量仅为0.39亿，显著降低了延迟和部署难度。该模型采用修剪的移动U-Net作为基础，通过在潜在空间中进行上下文空间拼接来统一条件。经过高质量的监督微调和强化学习，DreamLite在图像生成和编辑任务中表现优异，能够在小米14手机上以不到1秒的时间生成或编辑1024 x 1024的图像。

papers.title: HISA: Efficient Hierarchical Indexing for Fine-Grained Sparse Attention
papers.abstract: Abstract HISA improves sparse attention efficiency by replacing the traditional indexer with a hierarchical approach that reduces computational complexity from O(L²) to sub-quadratic scaling while maintaining selection fidelity.  AI-generated summary Token-level sparse attention mechanisms, exemplified by DeepSeek Sparse Attention (DSA), achieve fine-grained key selection by scoring every historical token for each query using a lightweight indexer, and then computing attention only over the selected subset. While the downstream sparse attention scales efficiently, the indexer still scans the entire prefix for every query, introducing an O(L^2) per-layer bottleneck that becomes prohibitive as context length grows. We propose HISA (Hierarchical Indexed Sparse Attention), a drop-in replacement for the indexer that transforms the search process from a flat token scan into a two-stage hierarchical procedure. First, a block-level coarse filter scores pooled block representatives to prune irrelevant regions. Then, a token-level refinement applies the original indexer only within the remaining candidate blocks. HISA preserves the exact token-level top-k sparsity pattern required by the downstream Sparse MLA operator and requires no additional training. On kernel-level benchmarks, HISA achieves a 2times speedup at 32K context length and 4times at 128K. On Needle-in-a-Haystack and LongBench, we directly replace the indexer in DeepSeek-V3.2 with HISA, without any fine-tuning. HISA closely matches the original DSA in quality while significantly outperforming block-sparse baselines. Moreover, the token selection sets produced by HISA and the original DSA exhibit a mean IoU greater than 99%, indicating that the efficiency gains come with virtually no impact on selection fidelity.

ru.title: Иерархический поиск для преодоления квадратичного узкого места в разреженном внимании
ru.desc: В статье предлагается HISA — иерархический подход для оптимизации механизма разреженного внимания в больших языковых моделях. Вместо сканирования всех токенов истории с временной сложностью O(L²), метод использует двухэтапную процедуру: сначала блочный фильтр отсеивает нерелевантные регионы, затем токен-уровневая фильтрация применяется только к оставшимся кандидатам. Предложенный подход не требует переобучения и может быть напрямую использован как замена в существующих моделях, достигая ускорения в 2-4 раза на длинных контекстах. Качество выбора токенов остаётся практически идентичным оригинальному методу с индексом пересечения наборов более 99%.

en.title: HISA: Hierarchical Efficiency for Sparse Attention
en.desc: The paper introduces HISA, a new method for improving the efficiency of sparse attention mechanisms in machine learning. By using a hierarchical approach, HISA reduces the computational complexity of the traditional indexer from O(L²) to a more efficient sub-quadratic scaling. This method involves a two-stage process where a coarse filter first eliminates irrelevant blocks, followed by a refined selection of tokens within the remaining blocks. HISA maintains the accuracy of token selection while significantly speeding up the process, achieving up to 4 times faster performance without additional training.

zh.title: 层次化索引，提升稀疏注意力效率
zh.desc: HISA（层次索引稀疏注意力）通过采用层次化的方法来提高稀疏注意力的效率，显著降低了计算复杂度，从O(L²)减少到亚二次规模，同时保持选择的准确性。该方法首先通过块级粗过滤器对汇总的块代表进行评分，以剔除不相关的区域，然后在剩余候选块内应用原始索引器进行细化。HISA在32K上下文长度下实现了2倍的加速，在128K上下文长度下实现了4倍的加速，且无需额外训练。实验结果表明，HISA在质量上与原始的DeepSeek稀疏注意力相近，同时在效率上显著优于块稀疏基线。

papers.title: MolmoPoint: Better Pointing for VLMs with Grounding Tokens
papers.abstract: Abstract A vision-language model approach for grounding that directly selects visual tokens containing target concepts through specialized pointing tokens, achieving superior performance in image, GUI, video pointing, and tracking tasks.  AI-generated summary Grounding has become a fundamental capability of vision-language models (VLMs). Most existing VLMs point by generating coordinates as part of their text output, which requires learning a complicated coordinate system and results in a high token count. Instead, we propose a more intuitive pointing mechanism that directly selects the visual tokens that contain the target concept. Our model generates a special pointing token that cross-attends to the input image or video tokens and selects the appropriate one. To make this model more fine-grained, we follow these pointing tokens with an additional special token that selects a fine-grained subpatch within the initially selected region, and then a third token that specifies a location within that subpatch. We further show that performance improves by generating points sequentially in a consistent order, encoding the relative position of the previously selected point, and including a special no-more-points class when selecting visual tokens. Using this method, we set a new state-of-the-art on image pointing (70.7% on PointBench), set a new state-of-the-art among fully open models on GUI pointing (61.1% on ScreenSpotPro), and improve video pointing (59.1% human preference win rate vs. a text coordinate baseline) and tracking (+6.3% gain on Molmo2Track). We additionally show that our method achieves much higher sample efficiency and discuss the qualitative differences that emerge from this design change.

ru.title: Прямой выбор визуальных токенов вместо координат для точного указания в видеоязыковых моделях
ru.desc: В работе предложен новый подход к грaunding'у в видеоязыковых моделях, который вместо генерации координат напрямую выбирает визуальные токены, содержащие целевые концепции. Модель использует специальные pointing-токены с кроссаттеншеном к входным изображениям или видео, обеспечивая многоуровневую точность за счет последовательного выбора областей с уточняющими токенами. Метод демонстрирует значительные улучшения на бенчмарках для указания точек, GUI-навигации и отслеживания объектов в видео. Предложенный подход превосходит существующие решения по точности и эффективности использования данных.

en.title: Revolutionizing Pointing in Vision-Language Models
en.desc: This paper presents a novel vision-language model that enhances the grounding process by using specialized pointing tokens to directly select visual tokens containing target concepts. Unlike traditional models that generate coordinates, which complicates the learning process, this approach simplifies pointing by allowing the model to cross-attend to image or video tokens. The model further refines its selections by using additional tokens to identify subpatches and specific locations within those patches. As a result, the proposed method achieves state-of-the-art performance in various tasks, demonstrating improved sample efficiency and qualitative outcomes.

zh.title: 直观指向，提升视觉-语言模型性能
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种视觉-语言模型的方法，通过专门的指向标记直接选择包含目标概念的视觉标记，从而在图像、图形用户界面（GUI）、视频指向和跟踪任务中实现了优越的性能。与现有模型通过生成坐标来指向不同，我们的方法采用更直观的指向机制，直接选择包含目标概念的视觉标记。该模型生成一个特殊的指向标记，能够交叉关注输入的图像或视频标记，并选择合适的标记。通过按顺序生成点并编码相对位置，我们的方法在图像指向和GUI指向等任务上设定了新的最先进的性能。

papers.title: MOOZY: A Patient-First Foundation Model for Computational Pathology
papers.abstract: Abstract A patient-first pathology foundation model named MOOZY uses a case transformer to model dependencies across multiple slides from the same patient, achieving superior performance on diverse clinical tasks through open, reproducible pretraining.  AI-generated summary Computational pathology needs whole-slide image (WSI) foundation models that transfer across diverse clinical tasks, yet current approaches remain largely slide-centric, often depend on private data and expensive paired-report supervision, and do not explicitly model relationships among multiple slides from the same patient. We present MOOZY, a patient-first pathology foundation model in which the patient case, not the individual slide, is the core unit of representation. MOOZY explicitly models dependencies across all slides from the same patient via a case transformer during pretraining, combining multi-stage open self-supervision with scaled low-cost task supervision. In Stage 1, we pretrain a vision-only slide encoder on 77,134 public slide feature grids using masked self-distillation. In Stage 2, we align these representations with clinical semantics using a case transformer and multi-task supervision over 333 tasks from 56 public datasets, including 205 classification and 128 survival tasks across four endpoints. Across eight held-out tasks with five-fold frozen-feature probe evaluation, MOOZY achieves best or tied-best performance on most metrics and improves macro averages over TITAN by +7.37%, +5.50%, and +7.83% and over PRISM by +8.83%, +10.70%, and +9.78% for weighted F1, weighted ROC-AUC, and balanced accuracy, respectively. MOOZY is also parameter efficient with 85.77M parameters, 14x smaller than GigaPath. These results demonstrate that open, reproducible patient-level pretraining yields transferable embeddings, providing a practical path toward scalable patient-first histopathology foundation models.

ru.title: Пациент в центре: foundation model для гистопатологии со слайд-зависимостями
ru.desc: MOOZY — это foundation model для гистопатологии, которая моделирует зависимости между несколькими слайдами одного пациента, используя case transformer вместо традиционного подхода, ориентированного на отдельные слайды. Модель обучена на открытых данных в два этапа: сначала vision encoder претренируется на 77 тысячах публичных слайдов методом masked self-distillation, затем case transformer выравнивается с клинической семантикой через мультизадачное обучение на 333 задачах. Модель показывает лучшие или сравнимые результаты с существующими подходами, улучшая метрики на 7-10% и имея в 14 раз меньше параметров, чем конкурирующие решения. Этот пациент-центричный подход демонстрирует, что открытое воспроизводимое обучение на уровне пациента создаёт переносимые представления для разнообразных клинических задач.

en.title: MOOZY: Transforming Pathology with Patient-Centric Modeling
en.desc: MOOZY is a novel pathology foundation model designed to prioritize patient cases over individual slides, addressing limitations in current computational pathology methods. It utilizes a case transformer to effectively model relationships among multiple slides from the same patient, enhancing performance across various clinical tasks. The model undergoes a two-stage pretraining process, first using masked self-distillation on a large dataset of slide feature grids, followed by aligning these representations with clinical semantics through multi-task supervision. MOOZY demonstrates superior performance metrics compared to existing models, while being more parameter efficient, showcasing the potential of patient-first approaches in histopathology.

zh.title: 以患者为中心的病理模型MOOZY
zh.desc: MOOZY是一种以患者为中心的病理基础模型，使用案例变换器来建模同一患者的多张切片之间的依赖关系。该模型通过开放和可重复的预训练，在多种临床任务中表现出色。MOOZY在预训练阶段结合了多阶段的自我监督和低成本的任务监督，显著提高了模型的性能。与现有方法相比，MOOZY在参数效率上也表现优异，展示了以患者为中心的病理模型的可扩展性。
